# doc-bricks MediaBrain

Canonical repository: https://github.com/doc-bricks/MediaBrain

## Summary

MediaBrain is a local-first PySide6 desktop application for managing personal media history, local files, streaming-provider entries, favorites, blacklists, tags, and playlists. It stores data locally in SQLite and configuration files. It has no telemetry, no tracking, and no automatic cloud sync.

## Primary Audience

- Users who want a private local media hub
- Developers looking for a PySide6 desktop media-library example
- Maintainers evaluating doc-bricks local-first desktop tools

## Key Files

- `README.md` - English public landing page
- `README_de.md` - German public landing page
- `PRIVACY_POLICY.md` - privacy boundaries and optional metadata calls
- `ARCH.md` - architecture overview
- `MediaBrain.py` - desktop application entry point
- `core.py` - media, tags, playlists, blacklist, and database logic
- `providers.py` - streaming and local-provider detection
- `gui.py` and `gui_playlists.py` - PySide6 user interface
- `tests/` - pytest regression and GUI smoke tests

## Search Phrases

- doc-bricks MediaBrain
- MediaBrain media library manager
- local-first PySide6 media manager
- privacy-first media history desktop app
- offline media hub smart playlists

## Boundaries

MediaBrain is not a streaming service, not a cloud sync product, and not an AI media generator. Optional metadata lookup depends on user-configured API keys or public metadata services.

## Last-checked: 2026-06-11
